name: Cephalopod
author: Mark Steere (impl. AGP)
description: Cephalopod (Mark Steere, 2006), a dice-stacking-by-capture majority game on a 5x5 grid. Each turn you add one die of your colour to a vacant square. If two, three, or four of the dice orthogonally adjacent to your placement have a pip sum of six or less, you MUST capture: choose such a subset (any qualifying subset of two or more adjacent dice summing to <=6), remove those dice, and your new die shows that pip sum. Otherwise the new die simply shows a one. There is no actual randomness: every die value is determined by play. When the board is completely full, the player owning the majority of the dice wins; on an odd-celled board no tie is possible.
